items.q: Why is the 4s orbital filled before the 3d orbital?
items.correct: Because the 4s orbital has a lower energy than 3d when both are empty, so electrons fill it first.
items.misconception: Because the 4s shell is closer to the nucleus than the 3d shell.
items.random_wrong: Because d orbitals can only fill after p orbitals in the next shell.
items.why: Students assume shell number directly correlates with distance/energy ordering, so 3-anything must come before 4-anything — but energy ordering crosses over at 4s/3d.

items.q: At dynamic equilibrium, what is happening to the reactions?
items.correct: Forward and reverse reactions continue at equal rates; concentrations remain constant.
items.misconception: Both reactions stop because the system has reached balance.
items.random_wrong: The forward reaction stops once the reverse reaction begins.
items.why: The word 'equilibrium' connotes stillness, so students think reactions stop — they miss that equilibrium is dynamic at the particle level.

items.q: Can you determine the order of a reaction with respect to a reactant from the balanced equation?
items.correct: No — orders must be determined experimentally; they often differ from the stoichiometric coefficients.
items.misconception: The order of reaction with respect to each reactant equals its coefficient in the balanced equation.
items.random_wrong: The order of reaction is always equal to the number of reactants in the equation.
items.why: Students see numbers in the balanced equation and try to reuse them — but rate laws depend on the mechanism, not just stoichiometry.
The answer examiners want is: CN⁻. “H⁺” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Members of a homologous series share:
items.correct: The same functional group and general formula
items.why: The answer examiners want is: The same functional group and general formula. “The same molecular mass” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
